item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Much less scary than Christmas decorations.

Actually, I'm taking this as a meta-commentary on the winter holidays creeping backwards every year. And in such a simple, almost classic-style skip with a fun twist, it's very fun. As all Wondertainment articles should be!

+1

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded: Fixed up the nitpicks, and I've altered the Discovery log significantly (primarily shortening it), so that it (hopefully) distracts less from the piece.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Okay, let's do this.

+ Nitpicks
– hide block

with its top hat removed

Maybe "removed and contained separately"? I imagine it being kept in a different chamber.

SCP-4745 is not to remain in its active state for more than six days at a time unless during testing

This sentence seems a little redundant, as it would only be in its active state at all during testing. The six day part doesn't seem necessary.

alter the abode to make it Halloween themed

Halloween-themed should be hyphenated like so.

SCP-4745 will change its theme to outright horror

Not a fan of this phrasing. Technically, while the anomaly is causing the stuff to happen, it's not "its theme". Not quite sure what you could change this to; try experimenting a little.

Manifestation of "Jason" from the Friday the 13th film franchise

I'd give Jason Voorhees his full name. Also, due to him not existing, I don't think he manifests. Maybe "Manifestation of an entity resembling…."?

It was at this point Foundation personnel became involved.

I think this flows a lot better with "that" after 'point'.

MTF Phi-13 "Spooky Scary Skeletons"

The MTF name should be in ("parentheses and quotation marks").

– hide block
+ Overall
– hide block

This was a difficult one to decide my opinion on. There are some things that I like, but also some things that I don't like. I'll start off with the good stuff:

I think that you have a pretty good understanding of how Wondertainment functions. While this is a defective product, you can see how it was intended to be a product for children, which they would enjoy. Also, some of this was pretty fun to read. I liked the brief list of manifestations, and the escalation of effects seemed like it was paced pretty well.

However, there was also some stuff that I didn't like. Most notably, the discovery log. It didn't really do anything special for me. Nothing out of the ordinary happened. She reacted in a completely reasonable way to what happened, as did law enforcement. The google search part feels awfully bolted on, and depending on your interpretation, either sort of overcomplicates this a little or doesn't really add much. I'm in the former group. How has this accessed the internet, and how does it have a completely unknown IP address. You've added too much anomalous ability without anything giving any implications as to why or how.

The biggest issue is that without it, I don't feel like there'd be quite enough here. The defective notice helps, but I feel that this needed something else. And a pretty procedural discovery log with an end which asks questions with no answers, for a reason which I don't find beneficial to the narrative, isn't quite the something else I'm looking for.

Overall, this is a novote for me as is, but I'm definitely seeing potential.

– hide block

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty rough around the edges. A little revising with the formatting and the narrative wouldn't hurt to fix some of the issues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep, you are doing this right!

And I wholeheartedly concur. This SCP has precisely the amount of spook I require for the spook season.

+🎃

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This really threw me for a loop. I mean I seriously thought it was just gonna be a really cute and simple one, and then it just hops right on over to the sp00ks. Never assume, I guess.

+1

(Also this is my first comment on this wiki can someone tell me if i'm doing this right?)

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Criticism
item.content:encoded:
I edited the clinical tone issues, and I'm especially glad you liked the series cancellation bit, since my initial motivation for this was actually a suspended Wondertainment series. Very glad that worked out.

Thanks for your thoughts!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made some edits, hopefully they clear things up a bit (I thought that the snowman just Google searching for scary things for inspiration would be funny).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Making Christmas. . . it's ours this time. +1

'There must have been some horror
In that old top hat they found
For when they placed it on his head
It began to spin around/

Carl the Snowman
Was as eldritch as could be
Spreading fear and gore
And so much more
Far as the eye can see'

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Criticism
item.content:encoded:
I quite liked this.

Tanhony rambles
TURN IT OFF

A few things that jumped out at me:

SCP-4745's alterations will begin to transition from child-friendly scariness

I’m not 100% here, but I feel like ‘scariness’ doesn’t quite fit with the tone. Maybe a different phrasing, like ‘horror elements’, could work better here?

and reports of frightening sounds and noises.

This seems a little too vague. A lot of different sounds can be frightening, so I feel like going just a tiny bit more specific with these would help the reader build up a strong mental image of what the anomaly is doing.

its alterations begin to revert at about twice the rate at which they initially appeared.

Again, the ‘about’ here doesn’t really seem to fit with the tone. Maybe ‘approximately’ or something along those lines would work better?

Find all the other members of this limited series and become the Holiday Hero!

I really love the fake-out you’ve done here. At first, I rolled my eyes, thinking that this would be an attempt to kick off a third Little Misters-esque series, but the subsequent cancellation of the series in the next addendum brought me back in.

Overall, I liked this quite a bit. Wondertainment’s always one of my favourite GOI’s, and I love seeing cases like this where it’s very clear that - even though the item did end up being dangerous - it was in no way intentional on Wondertainment’s part.

+1

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to Tanhony or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how it starts off Christmas-themed, and then, right when you don't expect it, it becomes Spooktober.
It reminds me of those people who dress up as Santa for Halloween.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this concept and the execution is solid. I do wish there was more detail in the description and addendum as to the nature of the more intense elements; you could do a lot to amplify the funny/weird/horror balance by leaning into some more granular descriptions.

Honestly those descriptions might help with what I think is the one gap here, which is whether the snowman's anomaly is due to generic malevolence or because the snowman is badly miscalibrating what's appropriate for the holiday season (like a haunted house for 13-year-olds that leans a little too much into Saw-esque imagery, cranked up many more notches).

Upvoted either way because spooky snowman and also because "with its top hat removed" is such a great narrative hook off the bat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, the point is that the series is discontinued because of the problems with this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MTF Phi-13 "Spooky Scary Skeletons"

If only you could upvote twice.

Are these wondertainment holiday scp's something you're planning for the future?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It looks like we're here to stay! Credit where credit's due:

First and foremost, huge thanks to MacWarren for coming up with the snowman part! Big thanks as well to gee0765, Simartar, Dyslexion, and cybersqyd for going over my concept and draft, helping me smooth out the edges.

And finally, big thanks to all of you for reading this!

Happy Spooktober, everyone!
